---
title: "points_visualization"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-18"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```



```{r}
source("../src/plot.R")
```

```{r plot-all-datasets, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(purrr)

# función de plot
plot_points_in_map <- function(df, color_by_class = FALSE, title = NULL, subtitle = NULL, caption = NULL) {
  
  p <- ggplot()
  
  if (color_by_class) {
    p <- p +
      geom_point(
        data = df,
        aes(x = decimalLongitude, y = decimalLatitude, color = factor(class)),
        size = 0.8
      ) +
      scale_color_manual(
        values = c("1" = "red", "0" = "blue"),
        name = "class"
      )
  } else {
    p <- p +
      geom_point(
        data = df,
        aes(x = decimalLongitude, y = decimalLatitude),
        color = "blue",
        size = 0.8
      )
  }
  
  p +
    coord_sf() +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(
      title = title,
      subtitle = subtitle,
      caption = caption
    )
}

# paths
output_dir <- "../../data/outputs/sdm_parallel"
manifest <- read_csv(file.path(output_dir, "manifest.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)

# recorrer corridas
for (i in seq_len(nrow(manifest))) {
  
  run <- manifest[i, ]
  dataset_path <- file.path(run$output_dir, "sdm_dataset_model_ready.csv")
  
  if (!file.exists(dataset_path)) next
  
  df <- read_csv(dataset_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
  
  p <- plot_points_in_map(
    df = df,
    color_by_class = TRUE,
    title = paste("Dataset:", run$species),
    subtitle = paste0(
      "bias = ", run$bias_method,
      if (!is.na(run$bias_param)) paste0(" (", run$bias_param, " km)") else "",
      " | bp = ", run$bp_method,
      " | bp_n_strategy = ", run$bp_n_strategy,
      " | bp_n = ", run$bp_n,
      " | env = ", run$env_set
    ),
    caption = paste0(
      "run_id: ", run$run_id,
      " | presencias: ", run$n_occ_after_bias,
      " | background: ", run$n_background
    )
  )
  
  print(p)
}
```
